description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer needs to determine the best PV array layout for a 10,000 m² commercial flat roof in Phoenix, AZ by comparing two strategies for total annual energy production.

Strategy A (Standard South): Fixed roof mount, 15-degree tilt, 180-degree azimuth, and a Ground Coverage Ratio (GCR) of 0.65.
Strategy B (East-West Interlocking): Fixed roof mount, high-density dual-tilt layout, GCR of 0.95, with half the capacity facing East (10-degree tilt, 90-degree azimuth) and half facing West (10-degree tilt, 270-degree azimuth).

Calculate the DC system capacities using a module efficiency of 0.20 kW/m² and simulate annual energy production using PySAM (Pvwattsv8) with the weather data at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv. Use the following simulation parameters: array_type = 1, losses = 14%, dc_ac_ratio = 1.2, and inv_eff = 96%. Save a JSON report to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/rooftop_layout_comparison.json with the following keys: capacity_strategy_A_kw, capacity_strategy_B_total_kw, annual_energy_strategy_A_kwh, annual_energy_strategy_B_total_kwh, and winning_strategy.
